title: rubus novae-cambriae
intro: Rubus novae-cambriae, also known as the New South Wales raspberry or New South Wales bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Rubus novae-cambriae include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Rubus novae-cambriae is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and has also been used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and promote tissue repair.
